summary.intent: The user wanted to add embedded terminal support to a tabbed Electron app (Gossamer) and then add a 'New Session' button that creates sessions inside the app's UI rather than spawning external windows.
summary.outcome: An EmbeddedTerminal xterm.js component backed by node-pty was added, a 'New Session' modal was built into ActiveSessions.tsx, and work began on switching from external window spawning to opening launcher tabs with embedded terminals running claude commands.

repo: Gossamer is an Electron app with a renderer (React/TypeScript) in app/src/renderer/src and a backend server in src/server.ts
Tab state is managed via TabsContext.tsx; tab types are extensible (session, repos, and now launcher)
Sessions have a repoRoot/cwd field used as the working directory for embedded terminals
spawnSession API exists on the server side and previously opened an external Terminal window running claude

code.finding: Tab types are defined here; a new 'launcher' tab type with cwd + command was being added at end of session
New Session modal added with repo Select dropdown and prompt Textarea; handleSpawn was being reworked to open a tab instead of calling spawnSession
node-pty WebSocket handler added to spawn real shells and bridge stdin/stdout/resize
xterm.js component with PTY WebSocket, resize drag handle, and minimize toggle; fill and initialCommand props were being added

workflow: The session was resumed mid-implementation; checking git diff and reading modified files was used to reconstruct context
Shell.tsx is the top-level tab renderer and is the integration point for new tab types

friction: Session was resumed from a previous conversation, requiring context reconstruction by reading modified files and git diffs
open_items: LauncherTab component in Shell.tsx not yet added — TabsContext was edited but Shell.tsx tab rendering integration was not completed
EmbeddedTerminal fill and initialCommand props were planned but may not have been fully implemented
handleSpawn in ActiveSessions.tsx needs to be fully switched from spawnSession API call to opening a launcher tab
No build/test run was done to verify the xterm+node-pty integration works end-to-end
